corpus.616d244b-f12b-4cb7-a5d0-138e67db5bff:
indicators suggested economic growth abroad remained subdued.  Although services activity and high-
tech manufacturing had been relatively robust, overall manufacturing activity remained weak, in part
corpus.e2dff607-87a9-46ce-8e05-4d33d8710199:
due to restrictive monetary policies.  Weakness in manufacturing was particularly pronounced in 
Canada, Germany, and Mexico.  In China, indicators of domestic demand remained weak.
corpus.429ac151-5496-4cbd-bbdf-571ff0016e2d:
Inflation in economies abroad continued to abate, on net, though developments were mixed.  In the 
AFEs excluding Japan, 12-month headline inflation ticked down but remained above target levels due
corpus.749f6021-71e4-4ac9-b09f-da3a770ccd2b:
to still-high services inflation.  In the emerging market economies, inflation moved sideways, with 
some Latin American economies still experiencing upward inflation pressures from food prices.  The
corpus.3f8c9fda-67b2-41e7-9a0c-adc5e8cf8eeb:
BOE cut its policy rate for the first time in the current cycle, while the BOC, the ECB, and the Bank of 
Mexico eased policy further, in part citing progress toward achieving their inflation targets.  By
corpus.904ebd5a-f52e-452e-82db-a3699b0cef04:
contrast, the BOJ continued to remove monetary accommodation. 
Staff Review of the Financial Situation 
The market-implied path for the federal funds rate declined notably over the intermeeting period.
corpus.b4a3c160-3eb3-4eb7-b1bd-0ba859925baf:
Similarly, options on interest rate futures suggested that market participants were placing higher odds 
on greater policy easing by early 2025 than they had just before the July FOMC meeting.  Consistent
corpus.0fe9a748-f697-44c9-a99f-0506b51c6bcc:
with the downward shift in the implied policy rate path, nominal Treasury yields declined significantly, 
on net, with the most pronounced decreases at shorter horizons driven by reductions in both inflation
corpus.cf60f591-cb74-4dde-8590-cf7d76d3d986:
compensation and real Treasury yields.  Market-based measures of interest rate uncertainty in the 
near term rose notably, reportedly reflecting in part increased concerns among investors about 
downside risks to economic activity.
corpus.8de3f637-2f6b-4c24-9c94-2f10cb03375e:
Broad stock price indexes increased, on net, despite a sizable but temporary drop in early August.  
Yield spreads on investment- and speculative-grade corporate bonds were little changed, on net, and
corpus.1a9652b8-e361-4c09-af3b-ec4296fa6ef3:
remained in the bottom quintile of their respective historical distributions.  The one-month option-
implied volatility on the S&P 500 index ended the period roughly unchanged, on net, after a large but 
temporary spike in early August.
corpus.da401ef7-74bd-4842-9daa-292cbf437e7d:
Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee    5 
 
Overnight secured rates were largely unchanged, and conditions in U.S. short-term funding markets 
remained stable.  Average usage of the ON RRP facility declined as net Treasury bill issuance
corpus.981d3bdf-90d6-4e54-a96b-75d592f9d0eb:
increased, providing a more attractive alternative asset for money market funds.   
Market-based measures of the expected paths of policy rates as well as sovereign bond yields in most
corpus.2e1f0cd4-b1a8-418c-9f5c-714b7f1dbc58:
AFEs fell notably, largely in response to declines in U.S. interest rates.  The broad dollar index declined, 
with the dollar depreciating significantly against AFE currencies amid a narrowing in interest rate
corpus.51e1a22d-4e9d-4022-9e6b-a5feba7bd162:
differentials between the U.S. economy and AFEs.  Financial markets were volatile early in the 
intermeeting period following the weaker-than-expected U.S. employment report and the policy rate
corpus.b34e1e59-a22a-49b2-9a3f-245b0391bf05:
increase by the BOJ, which led to the unwinding of some speculative trading positions.  However, 
declines in equities mostly retraced over the following weeks, and moves in foreign risky asset prices 
were mixed over the intermeeting period.
corpus.447e8911-c494-4091-a6f1-9b89d068f999:
In domestic credit markets, borrowing costs remained elevated despite modest declines in most credit 
segments.  Rates on 30-year conforming residential mortgages and yields on agency mortgage-backed
corpus.cb5f2744-5df7-427b-8628-06a95d1067a7:
securities (MBS) declined, on net, but continued to be elevated.  Interest rates on both new credit card 
offers and new auto loans were little changed and remained at elevated levels.  Interest rates for
corpus.9a80e82d-2f82-48e4-b406-066a7d1dcc74:
newly originated commercial real estate (CRE) loans on banks’ books increased.  Yields on an array of 
fixed-income securities, including investment- and speculative-grade corporate bonds and commercial
corpus.5c22b23e-8812-45ab-89d1-d1c2edab7b78:
mortgage-backed securities (CMBS), moved lower, generally following decreases in benchmark 
Treasury yields. 
Financing through capital markets and nonbank lenders was readily accessible for public corporations
corpus.a1af1010-01b9-472e-9849-01a22bc0aa25:
and for large and middle-market private corporations, and credit availability for leveraged loan 
borrowers remained solid.  For smaller firms, however, credit availability remained moderately tight.
corpus.09ee0dbd-10ae-493a-bbe3-7c8d9a702efa:
Commercial and industrial loan balances at banks were little changed on net.  Credit remained 
generally accessible to most CRE borrowers.  CRE loans at banks continued to decelerate in July and
corpus.aa59e93a-dd54-4c4a-9494-829a50eb2fd1:
were unchanged in August.  Non-agency CMBS issuance was robust in August, while agency CMBS 
issuance slipped to a bit below its post-pandemic average. 
Credit remained available for most consumers, though credit growth showed signs of moderating.
corpus.8c7cb97a-bcdf-4471-82d0-7639ac174667:
Auto lending continued to slow, while balances on credit cards increased moderately in July and 
August on average.  In the residential mortgage market, access to credit was little changed overall and
corpus.adfea7ae-548b-4794-9e5f-ee906e9d99b3:
continued to be sensitive to borrowers’ credit risk attributes. 
Credit quality continued to be solid for large and midsize firms, home mortgage borrowers, and
corpus.d7100931-c22a-4b8a-a38a-21a7e97ed81b:
municipalities but kept deteriorating in other sectors.  The credit quality of nonfinancial firms 
borrowing in the corporate bond and leveraged loan markets remained stable.  Delinquency rates on
corpus.0794e026-74a9-4e8f-a9b3-03e8ec88601f: loans to small businesses remained slightly above pre-pandemic levels.  Credit quality in the CRE
corpus.3445e8f9-07b7-43e3-804b-46c0b7f3d694:
6    September 17–18, 2024 
 
market deteriorated further, with the average delinquency rate for loans in CMBS and the share of 
nonperforming CRE loans at banks both rising further.  Regarding household credit quality,
corpus.0b1172c8-f46b-4c82-8855-1b815fb205a7:
delinquency rates on most residential mortgages remained near pre-pandemic lows.  Though 
consumer loan delinquency rates remained above pre-pandemic levels, the pace of increases had
corpus.3cfb2efd-514a-40bf-9ed9-903e2ae8aafe:
slowed.  Delinquency rates for credit cards rose moderately in the second quarter, while they were 
largely flat for auto loans. 
Staff Economic Outlook 
The staff forecast at the September meeting was for the economy to remain solid, with real GDP
corpus.75ee638c-ad5a-489c-af02-ae89e339deb4:
growth about the same as in the forecast for the July meeting but the unemployment rate a little 
higher.  Although real GDP growth in the second quarter was stronger than the staff had expected, the
corpus.0015d12a-72fe-471b-ae2f-4951541347fe:
forecast for economic growth in the second half of this year was marked down, largely in response to 
recent softer-than-expected labor market indicators.  The real GDP growth forecast for 2024 as a
corpus.b455103f-8ce8-40be-bc0a-007c78389d8d:
whole was little changed, though the unemployment rate was expected to be a little higher at the end 
of the year than previously forecast.  Over 2025 through 2027, real GDP growth was expected to rise
corpus.53f8c446-eef5-44b8-8125-72e3862dbfb6:
about in line with the staff’s estimate of potential output growth.  The unemployment rate was 
expected to remain roughly flat from 2025 through 2027.  All told, supply and demand in labor and
corpus.806fc117-42e9-4d5f-8e56-097419c355d3:
product markets were forecast to be more balanced and resource utilization less tight than they had 
been in recent years. 
The staff’s inflation forecast was slightly lower than the one prepared for the previous meeting,
corpus.512a690d-053d-4964-aa10-96ce5fcac3e1:
primarily reflecting incoming data, along with the projection of a less tight economy.  Both total and 
core PCE price inflation were expected to decline further as supply and demand in labor and product
corpus.03a0b537-0157-4073-83c0-e540280188be:
markets continued to move into better balance; by 2026, total and core inflation were expected to be 
2 percent. 
The staff judged that the risks around the baseline forecast for economic activity were tilted to the
corpus.ab07eb66-de21-4a96-88d4-f741518cbcd5:
downside, as the recent softening in some indicators of labor market conditions could point to greater 
slowing in aggregate demand growth than expected.  The risks around the inflation forecast were seen
corpus.da19f56e-ff24-42e1-b667-02affe122a0b:
as roughly balanced, reflecting both the further progress on disinflation and the effects of downside 
risks for economic activity on inflation.  The staff continued to view the uncertainty around the
corpus.90386f80-7c7d-4bab-9445-b2a40a181346:
baseline projection as close to the average over the past 20 years.   
Participants’ Views on Current Conditions and the Economic Outlook 
In conjunction with this FOMC meeting, participants submitted their projections of the most likely
corpus.eec034e6-07d3-467f-8f0b-90b4da43c81c:
outcomes for real GDP growth, the unemployment rate, and inflation for each year from 2024 through 
2027 and over the longer run.  These projections were based on participants’ individual assessments
corpus.a7bba1fa-a1a2-41a1-aa54-378614715628:
Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee    7 
 
of appropriate monetary policy, including their projections of the federal funds rate.  The longer-run
corpus.28105822-a8c9-4a29-85c4-0d447710f373:
projections represented each participant’s assessment of the rate to which each variable would tend 
to converge under appropriate monetary policy and in the absence of further shocks to the economy.
corpus.b6feb9ad-93af-492a-b4e4-b02e77828fbd:
The Summary of Economic Projections was released to the public after the meeting. 
In their discussion of inflation developments, participants observed that inflation remained somewhat
corpus.0cd95ff2-f7b7-4ef2-8776-12da5d8ce97a:
elevated, but almost all participants judged that recent monthly readings had been consistent with 
inflation returning sustainably to 2 percent.  Some participants commented that, though food and
corpus.64630107-2898-458f-8bf7-ed3192369947:
energy prices had played an important part in the decline in the overall inflation rate, slower rates of 
price increases had become more evident across a broad range of goods and services.  Notably, core
corpus.6398e46e-8e03-408d-afd2-f8240a8dfb38:
goods prices had declined in recent months, and the rate of increase in core nonhousing services 
prices had moved down further.  Many participants remarked that the recent inflation data were
corpus.7353f0ff-0d8c-4fc8-bc2a-35552e4e7e1f:
consistent with reports received from business contacts, who had indicated that their pricing power 
was limited or diminishing and that consumers were increasingly seeking discounts.  Many
corpus.4acfa694-d4cc-4ffa-b480-1ccd928b87e9:
participants also observed that inflation developments in the second and third quarters of 2024 
suggested that the stronger-than-anticipated inflation readings in the first quarter had been only a
